item.title: Why bring your own knife when Defender already left one in the kitchen? Extract credentials from PPL-protected LSASS using only Microsoft-signed components. No exploit is deployed. No driver is loaded
item.description: pWhy bring your own knife when Defender already left one in the kitchen? Extract credentials from PPL-protected LSASS using only Microsoft-signed components. No exploit is deployed. item.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
item.description: The infostealer was delivered via CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp, targeting credentials linking development and admin environments to wider enterprise systems.

item.title: RT by @Dinosn: We're back, analyzing CVE-2026-8037, a pre-auth RCE in Progress' Kemp LoadMaster appliance. https://labs.watchtowr.com/enterprise-tech-in-shell-out-progress-kemp-loadmaster-uninitialize

item.title: Iran, Russia, China Target Water Systems for Sabotage
item.description: Nation-state attackers breach water systems through weak passwords, exposed PLCs, and poor segmentation — not sophisticated malware.

item.title: You will design a seamless, low-friction KYC experience that reduces time-to-first-transaction while maintaining compliance integrity. Integrate a consistent identity layer, while balancing privacy, t
item.description: pYou will design a seamless, low-friction KYC experience that reduces time-to-first-transaction while maintaining compliance integrity.br /br /Integrate a consistent identity layer, while balancing privacy, trust, and global regulatory requirements./pimg src="https://nitter.net/pic/media%2FHL9gjhMW0

item.title: AMLBot Puts Polymarket Phishing Toll at $3.1M Across 11 Wallets, Funds Traced to Ethereum
item.description: Blockchain intelligence firm AMLBot has confirmed the Polymarket supply-chain attack total at approximately $3.1 million in PUSD across 11 user wallets, with funds bridged from Polygon to Ethereum and converted to ETH. Polymarket has pledged full refunds but has not named the compromised vendor.

item.title: RT by @polymutex: Walletbeat hit Berlin Blockchain Week 🇩🇪 Panels. Workshops. Interviews. One Devcon invite. We had a week 👀 Thanks to everyone who stopped to talk wallets, privacy, and standards with
item.description: pWalletbeat hit Berlin Blockchain Week 🇩🇪br /Panels. Workshops. Interviews. One Devcon invite.br /We had a week 👀br /br /Thanks to everyone who stopped to talk wallets, privacy, and standards with us 🙏🌸/pa href="https://nitter.net/walletbeat/status/2070867605326266807#m"br /Videobr / img src="https

item.title: Polymarket Confirms $3 Million Loss From Third-Party Front-End Supply-Chain Breach
item.description: Polymarket confirmed hackers drained approximately $3 million through a compromised third-party vendor that injected malicious code into the platform's website, and pledged full refunds to fewer than 15 affected accounts.

item.title: R to @l2beat: For more information, check our Privacy page 👇 https://l2beat.com/privacy/summary
item.description: pFor more information, check our Privacy page 👇br /br /a href="https://l2beat.com/privacy/summary"l2beat.com/privacy/summary/a/phr /bLink/bbr /a href="https://l2beat.com/privacy/summary"img src="https://nitter.net/pic/card_img%2F2070150171581612033%2FbL3NuY4h%3Fformat%3Dpng%26name%3D800x419" /br /bP

item.title: R to @l2beat: The top three privacy pools: Tornado Cash, Railgun, and Privacy pools, use zk-SNARKs (Groth16), which need a one-time ceremony to generate proving and verification parameters. That cerem
item.description: pThe top three privacy pools: Tornado Cash, Railgun, and Privacy pools, use zk-SNARKs (Groth16), which need a one-time ceremony to generate proving and verification parameters. That ceremony produces secret randomness - "toxic waste." If any party retains it, the setup is compromised. The ceremony's

item.title: Each privacy protocol can be evaluated from a CROPS perspective and when it comes to the S (Security), one thing that matters is trusted setup robustness. Our new Privacy page surfaces it for the pool
item.description: pEach privacy protocol can be evaluated from a CROPS perspective and when it comes to the S (Security), one thing that matters is trusted setup robustness. Our new Privacy page surfaces it for the pools we support.br /br /A short thread on what they are and why the numbers matter. 👇 🧵/pimg src="http
